message:
test(fcp-conformance/capability-promote): pin promote_with_instance contract incl. instance-agnostic case (br-i8175)

CapabilityToken<UnboundVerified>::promote_with_instance is the
gateway -> connector handoff and the typestate-level replay defense
for instance-bound tokens. Its documented contract has THREE
branches; jkcka_gateway_connector_handoff in fcp-host/tests covers
match + mismatch but NOT the third branch (instance-agnostic
tokens that promote unconditionally). That branch is a deliberate
security tradeoff and a future tightening would silently break
existing tokens unless this test is in place.

Adds 7 tests against the public fcp_core::CapabilityToken /
CapabilityVerifier API:

- promote_succeeds_when_instance_id_matches (case 1)
- promote_rejects_mismatched_instance_with_zone_violation (case 2,
  with assertions that the message names BOTH the claimed and
  expected instance ids so callers can route correctly)
- instance_agnostic_token_promotes_unconditionally (case 3,
  previously untested — pins the legacy "universally applicable"
  semantics)
- instance_agnostic_token_promotes_against_two_different_instances_independently
  (strengthens case 3: no implicit binding to first promote target)
- verify_bound_and_unbound_then_promote_yield_equivalent_claims
  (cross-path equivalence; gateway vs connector vantage must
  converge on the same enforcement)
- promoted_bound_token_carries_underlying_unbound_claims
  (promotion is a pure typestate transition; verified claims
  preserved)
- instance_mismatch_message_is_zone_violation_not_invalid_signature
  (variant-mapping contract: routing-layer error, NOT
  security-compromise error)

All 7 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
capability_promote_with_instance_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
